title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston Harbor is closing on March 29, after two years in operation.
The closure will affect 56 employees who recently voted to unionize, sparking claims of retaliatory motives from the union.
Seamark cited economic challenges as the reason for closing, despite expectations of improved market conditions in 2025 and 2026.

title: A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: St. Joseph School in Medford, serving pre-K through eighth grade, will close due to declining enrollment and rising costs.
Enrollment at St. Joseph School dropped from 222 in 2021-2022 to 169, making operations financially unsustainable.
The school, founded by Sisters of Saint Joseph in 1929, will offer assistance for student transfers and prioritize staff reemployment.

title: This 14-time marathoner is running the Boston Marathon for his daughter and children’s health
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Chris Ruggio, 57, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Boston Children's Hospital Miles for Miracles team.
Ruggio's daughter, Holly, has juvenile idiopathic arthritis, and he aims to support children's health charities through his runs.
Ruggio has raised over $70,000 for children's health charities and hopes to earn the Abbott World Marathon Majors Six Star Medal.

title: A 15-year-old girl was found dead in a Plymouth forest in 1986. Her alleged killer is finally standing trial.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, is on trial for the 1986 murder of Tracy Gilpin, found dead in Myles Standish State Forest.
Hand was arrested in 2018 in North Carolina; he faces first-degree murder charges and has pleaded not guilty.
Defense argues interrogation methods; Hand claimed an accident but allegedly admitted to moving a rock that caused Gilpin's death.

title: Healey demands $1,745 tariff refunds for each Mass. household
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey demands nearly $4.9 billion in tariff refunds for Massachusetts residents following Supreme Court's ruling.
The Supreme Court ruled Trump's tariffs unconstitutional, prompting Healey's call for $1,745 refunds per household.
A federal judge ruled that companies owed refunds, but the process for consumer compensation remains unclear.

title: Mattapan store owner pleads guilty in $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mattapan store owner Antonio Bonheur pleaded guilty to $6.7M SNAP fraud involving fraudulent food assistance transactions.
From 2021-2025, Bonheur exchanged SNAP benefits for cash, ineligible items, and sold prohibited goods at Jesula Variety Store.
Bonheur's operations led to a spike in SNAP redemptions, with monthly transactions reaching over $540,000 at their peak.

title: JetBlue is cutting a route between Boston and this southern city
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue is discontinuing its seasonal route between Boston and Asheville, North Carolina, due to underperformance.
The route, launched in 2022, was planned to resume in May but has now been permanently canceled.
Affected passengers will receive full refunds, and JetBlue plans to focus on more sustainable routes.

title: Theater review: ‘Stereophonic’ at Emerson Colonial really makes you feel like you’re with the band
author: Jackie Chianca
bullets: "Stereophonic" runs at Emerson Colonial Theatre in Boston until March 15, featuring a rock band's recording struggles.
The play, set in a 1970s recording studio, combines character studies with music by Arcade Fire's Will Butler.
Directed by Daniel Aukin, the production explores band dynamics and showcases the immersive, tension-filled artistic process.

title: Joe Mazzulla referenced his high school principal while defending Jaylen Brown after ejection
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected in the second quarter during a Celtics vs. Spurs game after receiving two technical fouls.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla supported Brown post-ejection, referencing his high school principal to criticize officiating decisions.
The Celtics lost the game 125-116 to the San Antonio Spurs, with a follow-up game against the Thunder in Oklahoma City.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest movement announces March rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The 'No Kings' rally is set for March 28 from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m. at Boston Common.
Organizers include the ACLU of Massachusetts and Indivisible Mass Coalition, opposing authoritarianism and promoting democracy.
Past events drew over 100,000 attendees; organizers expect the upcoming rally to be the largest yet.
